conversations\\n| * 7b6cfcc chore(sync): add Development_Docs workflow\\n# Codex handoff\\n\\n- Canonical repository: `Kgray44/treasurehuntSoT`\\n- Integrated branches: `feature/game-master-command-center` and `feature/cinematic-animation-rebuild`\\n- Integration method: normal merge commits on `main`, preserving both source histories\\n- Status: expanded Command Center, complete Player Companion, cinematic animation architecture, and Tall Tale Studio share one integrated tree\\n- Command Center routes: `/quartermaster` plus `/chapters`, `/hints`, `/voyage`, `/artifacts`, `/quests`, `/journal`, `/events`, `/player-view`, `/recovery`, `/audit`, `/diagnostics`\\n- Authentication: GM bcrypt/database session and CSRF; player sessions cannot call admin APIs\\n- Commands: prepare/release/solve/complete chapter, prepare/release hint, reveal map, award artifact, discover/advance quest, release journal entry, pause/resume, undo, reconciliation\\n- Migration: Phase 2 `20260716223000_player_companion_shell`, then Phase 3 `20260716233000_game_master_command_center`; connector-parity MySQL SQL exists and is wired through `npm run db:migrate:mysql:command-center`\\n- Development data: Phase 2 multi-state presets plus Phase 3 prepared-action and audit fixtures; normal launcher restarts preserve campaign state, while `npm run db:preset -- <preset>` is the explicit reset path\\n- Startup: `npm run dev:full`; validation: `npm run validate`; stop: `npm run dev:stop`\\n- Known limitations: no background scheduler, multi-event rollback, automated presence cleanup, Redis fan-out, real finale/story/private media, or deployment\\n- Latest full validation: 19 unit tests, 10 executed browser tests with 2 intentional shared-database skips, 16 accepted progression events, 18 audit records, preserved sequence after seed ensure, production build, and two production restart proofs\\n- Source pull requests: `https://github.com/Kgray44/treasurehuntSoT/pull/1` and `https://github.com/Kgray44/treasurehuntSoT/pull/2`\\n- Required gate: complete post-merge validation and synchronize project records before pushing `main`\\n\\n## Cinematic and Studio handoff\\n\\n- Source branch: `feature/cinematic-animation-rebuild`\\n- Baseline commit: `2c07497f148707a72eac15783b88293ff0b3413d`\\n- Phase completed: cinematic animation architecture and full interface rebuild\\n- Version: `0.2.0`\\n- Repository visibility: **public** as verified 2026-07-17; only fictional development content is permitted\\n- Updated: 2026-07-17\\n\\n## Product surfaces\\n\\n- Landing: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/`\\n- Player: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/tale/development-forever-treasure`\\n- Game Master: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/quartermaster`\\n- Development showcase: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/dev/animations`\\n- Player sections: `journal`, `chart`, `treasures`, `quests`, `log`, `finale`\\n\\nThe landing, access gate, player journal workspace, chart, treasure altar, quest ledger, ship's log, finale chamber, and quartermaster dashboard share the physical moonlit-journal design system. The development showcase proves every authored scene, runtime control, fallback, motion mode, and the serial trailer without calling progression APIs. The showcase route returns 404 in production.\\n\\n## Animation architecture\\n\\n- `AnimationDirector` is the single transport for scene registration, playback, pause, resume, seek, speed, skip, reverse, cancellation, cleanup, and observable state.\\n- GSAP owns authored timeline transforms, SVG drawing, masks, SplitText, MotionPath, labels, and callbacks.\\n- Motion owns React mount/unmount, layout, navigation, dialog, and press interactions.\\n- StPageFlip owns physical page turns over a shared React page model; reduced motion uses a semantic static page reader.\\n- Rive is dynamically loaded from a local WebGL2 binary with CDN fallback disabled and SVG failure fallbacks.\\n- Lottie is dynamically loaded from three original local JSON assets and exposes play, pause, segment, speed, direction, visibility, and destruction controls.\\n- Full, gentle, and reduced policies preserve state and semantic reading order. Document and element visibility pause expensive runtimes.\\n- Procedural Web Audio cues are optional, user-unlocked, disposable, and never block navigation or state transitions.\\n\\nSee `docs/animation/` for scene inventory, ownership rules, asset contracts, provenance, showcase operation, testing, and performance notes.\\n\\n## Data and security boundaries\\n\\n- Existing Prisma, session, allowlist projection, SSE, event ordering, and audit boundaries remain intact.\\n- The showcase uses deterministic fictional props and makes no player or GM mutation requests.\\n- Local animation assets are validated for expected paths, JSON shape, Rive magic bytes, and remote URL exclusion.\\n- No production secrets, private story content, photographs, analytics, or new third-party network calls were added.\\n\\n## Development commands\\n\\n- Start: `npm run dev:full`\\n- Stop: `npm run dev:stop`\\n- Full validation: `npm run validate`\\n- Animation asset validation: `npm run assets:validate`\\n- Unit tests: `npm test`\\n- Production build: `npm run build`\\n\\nOn a UNC checkout, the PowerShell scripts mirror into `%LOCALAPPDATA%/ForeverTreasureCompanion` so Node, Prisma, Next.js, and Playwright operate on a local filesystem. Local development defaults remain `development-moonwake` for the player and `kato` / `development-captain-only` for the GM; never reuse them outside the disposable local database.\\n\\n## Validation state\\n\\n- Strict TypeScript: passing\\n- ESLint: passing with zero findings\\n- Formatting: passing\\n- Animation asset validator: 3 Lottie JSON files, 1 Rive binary, and local SVG fallbacks passing\\n- Unit tests: 50 passing across 16 files\\n- In-app browser: responsive landing/access/player/GM/showcase flows inspected; clean showcase load has no console warnings or errors\\n- Showcase diagnostics: 60 FPS idle estimate, zero-to-one initial development long task depending on cache, expected 1 Rive / 2 Lottie / 1 PageFlip full-mode steady-state counts, no asset failures\\n- Horizontal overflow: none at 390, 1440, or 2560 CSS pixels in the showcase audit\\n- Full `npm run validate`: passing on 2026-07-17 with 8 applicable Playwright tests passing, 2 intentional heavy-suite project skips, 8 ordered events/audits verified, optimized production build passing, two production restarts passing, and the development showcase returning 404 in production\\n- Validation artifacts: `%LOCALAPPDATA%/ForeverTreasureCompanion/validation/artifacts/validation`\\n\\n## Remaining production work\\n\\n- Replace the documented development Rive sample and all fictional copy with commissioned/private production art and story content after the repository becomes private.\\n- Profile real production art on target low-end mobile hardware and repeat memory traces after sustained navigation.\\n- SSE fan-out remains process-local; add Redis or equivalent before multi-instance deployment.\\n- Exercise the parallel MySQL migration against MySQL 8 in CI.\\n- The finale remains a safe shell until private requirements and ending content are authored.\\n\\r\\n# The Forever Treasure Companion\\n\\nProduction-oriented foundation and fully automated local demo for a private nautical-fantasy date-night Tall Tale. All committed story material is generic development seed content; no final surprise content belongs in this public repository.\\n\\n## Current phase\\n\\nThe cinematic companion now also includes Tall Tale Studio Phase 1: an authenticated no-code authoring workspace, reusable asset/location/artifact libraries, immutable publishing, a dynamic player catalog, version-pinned live sessions, and Captain controls. A shared animation director still coordinates the original harbor, journal, chart, artifact, and finale experience without allowing presentation timing to outrun server truth. All committed content remains fictional development seed material.\\n\\nThe Phase 3 feature branch adds an expanded Game Master Command Center: 12 URL-backed workspaces, truthful presence/synchronization, sanitized Player View, nonmutating projection, staging, idempotent commands, stale-tab protection, recovery, audit/diagnostics, keyboard controls, and tablet/emergency layouts. It remains unintegrated until the parallel Phase 2 reconciliation pass.\\n\\n## Stack\\n\\n- Next.js 16 App Router, React 19, strict TypeScript\\n- Prisma 6.19 with SQLite for local/test and an equivalent MySQL production schema\\n- GSAP 3.15 for orchestrated scenes and SVG/text work; Motion 12 for React interaction and presence\\n- StPageFlip for the journal; local Rive and Lottie runtimes with explicit static fallbacks\\n- bcrypt password/access-code hashing, server-side database sessions, CSRF tokens, and rate limiting\\n- Vitest and Playwright\\n\\n## Reproducible setup\\n\\nRequirements: Windows PowerShell 5.1+, Node.js 24, npm 11, and Git. MySQL 8 is only required for production parity; the supported local/demo/test strategy is SQLite.\\n\\n### Fastest Windows demo\\n\\nAfter cloning, double-click `Start Forever Treasure Dev.cmd`, or run:\\n\\n```powershell\\nnpm run dev:full\\n```\\n\\nThe launcher creates an ignored `.env` when absent, installs the exact lockfile, generates Prisma, applies migrations, creates generic development data only when the voyage is missing, starts the app, checks health, and prints the URLs and credentials. Existing campaign progress is preserved across normal stop/start cycles. Network/UNC workspaces are mirrored to an ignored local runtime under `%LOCALAPPDATA%` because Node and SQLite are unreliable on network shares. Stop it with `npm run dev:stop`.\\n\\n- Player: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/tale/development-forever-treasure`\\n- Tall Tale catalog: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/tales`\\n- Player phrase: `development-moonwake`\\n- GM: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/quartermaster`\\n- Studio: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/studio`\\n- Captain: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/captain`\\n- GM development[REDACTED: LOGIN CREDENTIAL]\\n\\nThese values are disposable local defaults, never production credentials. To opt into LAN access, run `powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts/start-dev.ps1 -Lan`; Windows Firewall and network policy still determine whether another device can connect.\\n\\n### Portable manual setup\\n\\n```bash\\ngit clone https://github.com/Kgray44/treasurehuntSoT.git\\ncd treasurehuntSoT\\nnpm ci\\ncp .env.example .env\\nnpm run db:generate\\nnpm run db:migrate\\nnpm run db:seed\\nnpm run dev\\n```\\n\\nSet unique local values in `.env`; the seedâ€™s fallback values are development-only and must never be used outside an isolated development database. Player path: `/tale/development-forever-treasure`. GM path: `/quartermaster`.\\n\\n## Commands\\n\\n| Command                                   | Purpose                                                               |\\n| ----------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------- |\\n| `npm run dev:full`                        | Idempotent setup, migration, progress-safe seed check, and server     |\\n| `npm run dev:stop`                        | Safely stop only the recorded development process                     |\\n| `npm run validate`                        | Fresh DB, format/lint/types/unit/E2E/a11y/build/restart proof         |\\n| `npm run dev`                             | Next.js only; assumes setup is already complete                       |\\n| `npm run build`                           | Production build                                                      |\\n| `npm start`                               | Start production build                                                |\\n| `npm test`                                | Domain, command-center, Studio, animation, and component unit tests   |\\n| `npm run assets:validate`                 | Validate local Lottie/Rive contracts and fallback paths               |\\n| `npm run test:e2e`                        | Cross-browser player, Command Center, Studio, and animation workflows |\\n| `npm run db:migrate`                      | Apply local SQLite migrations                                         |\\n| `npm run db:seed`                         | Load replaceable development content                                  |\\n| `npm run db:preset -- mid-voyage`         | Reset to a repeatable development-only companion preset               |\\n| `npm run db:generate:mysql`               | Generate production MySQL client                                      |\\n| `npm run db:migrate:mysql:command-center` | Apply the Phase 3 production MySQL migration                          |\\n| `npm run db:migrate:mysql:studio`         | Apply the production-parity Tall Tale Studio migration                |\\n\\nFull setup, validation stages, output locations, clean-clone instructions, and troubleshooting are in [local development](docs/local-development.md) and [testing](docs/testing.md). Studio architecture and operations are in [Tall Tale Studio](docs/tall-tale-studio.md); the future recognition seam is in [vision helper boundary](docs/future-vision-helper.md). Animation ownership, scene contracts, assets, performance, and the development lab are indexed from [animation architecture](docs/animation/architecture.md).\\n\\n## Repository workflow\\n\\n`main` is canonical. At task start: fetch, inspect status/remotes, and pull with rebase when clean. At task end: validate, review the complete diff, commit intentionally, fetch/rebase if needed, push without force, and verify local `HEAD` equals `origin/main`. See [Codex handoff](docs/codex-handoff.md).\\n\\n## Important limitations\\n\\nThe full story, final Rive artwork/audio, horizontal scale-out for SSE, and production deployment are later work. The current sound is restrained and procedural. The sole third-party visual sample is MIT-licensed, local, documented, and development-only; production uses original SVG/CSS fallbacks. The repository is public, so only generic development content is permitted. See [known limitations](docs/known-limitations.md).\\n\\n## Next steps\\n\\nAdd CI for the complete validation gate, exercise the parallel MySQL schema in an integration environment, and replace process-local SSE fan-out before multi-instance deployment. Real surprise content remains blocked until repository visibility is verified private.\\n\\r\\n\"}", "type": "input_text"}]

Preserve working functionality. \r\nAdapt the architecture to the actual stack instead of replacing the project with a disconnected rewrite.\r\nRequired working behavior\r\n Work from the current repository state. Do not assume a framework, database, deployment target, or component library \r\nuntil the repository proves it.\r\n Create an implementation inventory first. Identify the existing Player page, Captain page, Studio page, current password \r\nbehavior, Tall Tale records, active playthrough state, media storage, and any current authentication/session logic.\r\n Build incrementally. Introduce migrations, compatibility adapters, and route redirects where necessary. Do not destroy \r\ncurrent Tall Tales or invalidate current Player and Captain URLs without a safe transition path.\r\n Use server-enforced authorization. Hiding buttons is presentation only. Every protected page, API request, asset fetch, \r\nmutation, realtime channel, and export must be authorized on the server.\r\n Prefer polished completeness over scaffolding. Do not leave placeholder buttons, dead navigation, fake status text, hard-\r\ncoded sample progress, or unimplemented invitation actions.\r\n Verify the complete user journey. Run tests and manually exercise the role gateway, Player sign-in, Captain sign-in, Creator \r\nentry, invitation creation, invitation acceptance, voyage start, progress continuation, completion, and historical replay views.\r\nExpected deliverables from Codex\r\n[ ] Repository-aware implementation plan recorded in the project documentation.\r\n[ ] Database migrations and seed/backfill logic that preserve existing Tall Tales and playthroughs.\r\n[ ] Role gateway and mode-specific authentication flows.\r\n[ ] Player Tall Tale Library with invited, ready, active, completed, and historical playthrough states.\r\n[ ] Captain Library, invitation wizard, invitation management, and active-voyage launch controls.\r\n[ ] Creator/Studio entry and role-aware workspace switching.\r\n[ ] Immutable published Tale versions and immutable playthrough-version binding.\r\n[ ] Server-side role, resource, field, and asset authorization.\r\n[ ] Responsive, accessible, cinematic frontend behavior with reduced-motion support.\r\n[ ] Automated unit, integration, authorization, and end-to-end tests.\r\n[ ] Updated developer documentation, route map, data model documentation, and migration notes.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 3 =====\r\nTALL TALE PLATFORM\r\nCODEX IMPLEMENTATION SPECIFICATION  |  JULY 17, 2026 PAGE 3\r\n02  PRODUCT VISION AND NON-NEGOTIABLE \r\nPRINCIPLES\r\nThe platform must feel like a deliberate adventure experience while behaving like a disciplined multi-role application underneath.\r\nProduct vision\r\nThe website must become the central Tall Tale platform. A visitor first enters through a cinematic role gateway, then receives a \r\ncompletely different experience depending on whether they enter as Player, Captain, or Creator. The system underneath must \r\nremain unified so Tall Tales, published versions, playthroughs, invitations, assets, and history are never duplicated across \r\ndisconnected applications.\r\nThe six non-negotiable principles\r\nPrinciple Required interpretation\r\nOne platform, three experiences Player, Captain, and Creator use shared domain data but receive different navigation, permissions, \r\ncopy, and workflows.\r\nRole selection is not \r\nauthorization Selecting Captain or Creator only opens that authentication path. It never grants access.\r\nThe Player owns a library \r\nexperience\r\nPlayers can see every Tall Tale they were invited or added to, every active voyage, every completed \r\nvoyage, and the exact historical version associated with each completed playthrough.\r\nPublished history is immutable A playthrough remains bound to the exact published version used when it began. Later edits cannot \r\nrewrite the player's past.\r\nSpoilers are protected as data\r\nFuture chapters, hidden assets, Captain instructions, unrevealed titles, logic conditions, and private \r\nnotes must never be returned to Player clients before authorization and reveal conditions permit \r\nthem.\r\nCinematic does not mean \r\ndishonest\r\nAnimations may be elaborate, but statuses, progress, presence, invitation state, and synchronization \r\nindicators must reflect real backend truth.\r\nPRODUCT SMELL TO PREVENT\r\nDo not create three separate databases, three separate Tall Tale copies, or three unrelated application shells. The entire \r\npurpose of the library is to make every experience feel connected while permissions determine what each person is \r\nallowed to see and do.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 4 =====\r\nTALL TALE PLATFORM\r\nCODEX IMPLEMENTATION SPECIFICATION  |  JULY 17, 2026 PAGE 4\r\n03  INFORMATION ARCHITECTURE\r\nThe library is the organizing center; the role gateway is the front door; Player, Captain, and Creator are role-specific workspaces.\r\nTop-level route families\r\n/\r\n/player\r\n/player/sign-in\r\n/player/library\r\n/join/:invitationToken\r\n/play/:playthroughId\r\n/captain\r\n/captain/sign-in\r\n/captain/library\r\n/captain/tales/:taleId\r\n/captain/voyages/:playthroughId\r\n/captain/invitations\r\n/studio\r\n/studio/sign-in\r\n/studio/library\r\n/studio/tales/:taleId\r\n/studio/tales/:taleId/versions\r\n/studio/tales/:taleId/publish\r\nThe exact path syntax may follow the current framework, but the conceptual route families must remain distinct. The Player \r\napplication must not contain visible Captain or Studio navigation. Captain and Creator may share an authenticated account and \r\nswitch authorized workspaces, but the Player experience must remain fully isolated.\r\nWorkspace relationships\r\nWorkspace Primary purpose Default landing area May expose\r\nPublic Gateway Choose entry intent Role selection scene Player, Captain, Creator entry choices\r\nPlayer Experience and remember voyages Player Tall Tale Library Only player-safe Tall Tale and \r\nplaythrough data\r\nCaptain Operate voyages and manage players Captain Library / Command Authorized Tale controls, invitations, \r\nplayer progress, Captain notes\r\nCreator Author and publish content Studio Library Drafts, media, logic, validation, \r\nversions, publishing\r\nTall Tale details page\r\nEvery Tall Tale must have a central details page appropriate to the current workspace. The underlying Tale identity is shared, \r\nwhile the visible tabs and actions vary by role.\r\n Player details: spoiler-safe overview, invitations, active run, completed runs, memory archive, exact version played, collected \r\nmedia, revealed artifacts, and replay availability.\r\n Captain details: overview, published versions, active voyages, invited players, completed playthroughs, readiness, Captain \r\npreview, launch actions, and operational history.\r\n Creator details: draft health, chapters, block graph, media, logic, tests, publishing, version history, comparisons, restore/fork \r\noperations, and playthrough compatibility warnings.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 5 =====\r\nTALL TALE PLATFORM\r\nCODEX IMPLEMENTATION SPECIFICATION  |  JULY 17, 2026 PAGE 5\r\n04  CINEMATIC ROLE SELECTION GATEWAY\r\nThe first page must feel like starting a game, not choosing a department from a corporate intranet.\r\nOpening scene\r\nBuild a full-screen, responsive opening gateway that introduces the world before asking for credentials. The preferred scene is a \r\ndim captain's cabin or weathered chart table emerging from darkness and ocean ambience. The visual treatment must match the \r\nestablished Tall Tale art direction and must not introduce a generic fantasy theme.\r\n1. Begin with darkness and subtle ocean or ship ambience, respecting prior audio consent and browser autoplay restrictions.\r\n2. Reveal a horizon glow, lantern light, or moving water reflection.\r\n3. Bring the role objects into view through layered motion rather than a single opacity fade.\r\n4. Allow interaction as soon as the controls are understandable; do not make the visitor endure an unskippable cinematic every \r\nvisit.\r\n5. Remember returning sessions and provide a fast path while preserving a visible Return to Role Selection control outside the \r\nPlayer experience.\r\nRole objects and interaction\r\nRole Visual object Supporting copy Interaction behavior\r\nPlayer Open journal, compass, or \r\nsealed invitation\r\nContinue an adventure, accept an invitation, or revisit \r\ncompleted voyages.\r\nPages stir, compass responds, \r\ninvitation seal glows.\r\nCaptain Ship wheel, chart table, key, or \r\ncommand lantern\r\nGuide voyages, invite players, reveal clues, and control the \r\njourney.\r\nLantern brightens, charts \r\nalign, navigation marks \r\nappear.\r\nCreator Quill, ink bottle, drafting tools, \r\nloose maps Create, test, publish, and preserve Tall Tales. Ink draws structures and \r\nsketches into the map.\r\nTwo-role fallback\r\nImplement the data and permission model for all three roles even if the current design temporarily displays only Player and \r\nCaptain. If only two buttons are shown, the authenticated Captain workspace must provide an explicit Open Studio or Switch to \r\nCreator Workspace action for accounts with Creator permission. Do not collapse Creator authorization into frontend visibility \r\nassumptions.\r\nReturning visitor behavior\r\n Returning Player: show Continue [Tall Tale Name] when exactly one active voyage exists, or Open My Tall Tale Library when \r\nmultiple items require selection.\r\n Returning Captain: show Return to Captain's Command and a truthful summary such as one player awaiting launch or two \r\ninvitations pending.\r\n Returning Creator: show Continue Editing [Tall Tale Name] when a recent draft is known, plus Open Studio Library.\r\n Expired session: retain the selected intent and return the user to the matching sign-in screen without exposing protected \r\ndetails.\r\nAnimation requirements\r\n Use layered transforms, lighting, depth, cloth/paper response, water reflection, and object-specific motion. Avoid a ten-\r\nsecond opacity fade pretending to be cinematic.\r\n Provide skip, reduced-motion, and replay controls where appropriate.\r\n Do not block keyboard navigation or screen-reader access while visual animation runs.\r\n Pause or simplify expensive effects when the tab is hidden, the device is low-power, or reduced motion is enabled.\r\n Ensure the gateway reaches a stable, usable state after navigation back, refresh, session restore, and browser history \r\ntraversal.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 6 =====\r\nTALL TALE PLATFORM\r\nCODEX IMPLEMENTATION SPECIFICATION  |  JULY 17, 2026 PAGE 6\r\n05  AUTHENTICATION, SESSIONS, AND WORKSPACE \r\nINTENT\r\nAuthentication identifies the person; authorization determines the role; workspace intent determines which authorized interface \r\nopens.\r\nPlayer authentication\r\nPreserve the current simple Player password experience while evolving it into a real Player identity and invitation model. A \r\nPlayer may enter through an invitation link, short invitation code, remembered session, or direct Player sign-in. The platform \r\nmust support lightweight access without requiring every invited person to create a full email-based account immediately.\r\n Invitation entry: the token identifies a specific invitation and playthrough. Ask only for the configured PIN/password or \r\nidentity confirmation.\r\n Direct Player sign-in: support Player display name/username plus password, or the current password-only flow when the \r\nplayer identity is already established by URL, device, invitation, or existing session.\r\n Guest Player profile: allow a playthrough-bound profile that can later be claimed by a full account without losing history.\r\n Remembered session: use secure, revocable server-side sessions or securely signed session tokens. Do not store raw \r\npasswords, invitation tokens, or authorization claims in local storage.\r\nCaptain and Creator authentication\r\nCaptain and Creator access must use stronger account authentication than a lightweight Player PIN. Use the repository's existing \r\nauthentication stack where present. Otherwise implement username/email plus password with modern password hashing, \r\nsecure cookies, login throttling, and a future-compatible authentication-method table for passkeys or external providers.\r\nWorkspace intent rules\r\nSelected entry Authentication result Authorized destination\r\nPlayer Valid Player identity or invitation Player Library or invitation acceptance flow\r\nCaptain Account with Captain permission Captain Library / Command\r\nCaptain Authenticated account without Captain \r\npermission Safe access-denied page; no Captain data\r\nCreator Account with Creator permission Studio Library\r\nCreator Account with Captain but not Creator \r\npermission\r\nSafe no-Studio-access message and return to authorized \r\nworkspace\r\nAny protected \r\nroute No valid session Matching sign-in screen with safe return path\r\nCRITICAL AUTHORIZATION RULE\r\nNever trust role, Tale ID, playthrough ID, player ID, chapter ID, asset ID, or invitation state supplied by the browser. \r\nResolve every permission from the authenticated server-side principal and current database state.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 7 =====\r\nTALL TALE PLATFORM\r\nCODEX IMPLEMENTATION SPECIFICATION  |  JULY 17, 2026 PAGE 7\r\n06  AUTHORIZATION AND SPOILER-SAFE DATA \r\nISOLATION\r\nSecurity must operate at route, resource, action, field, asset, and realtime-channel levels.\r\nRole and resource model\r\nCapability Player Captain Creator Administrator\r\nView authorized Player library Yes Preview only Preview only Yes\r\nView Captain workspace No Assigned \r\nTales/voyages Optional by permission Yes\r\nOperate playthrough Player actions \r\nonly Assigned voyages No unless also Captain Yes\r\nCreate/edit draft No Optional Assigned Tales Yes\r\nPublish version No Optional policy Authorized \r\ncreator/publisher Yes\r\nView authoring version history No Authorized Tale only Authorized Tale only Yes\r\nView completed version played Yes, for own run Assigned Tale/run Authorized Tale/run Yes\r\nManage invitations Accept/decline \r\nown Authorized voyages No unless also Captain Yes\r\nPlayer-safe projection\r\nCreate a deliberate Player-safe data projection instead of serializing complete Tall Tale objects and hiding fields in \r\nReact/Vue/Svelte templates. The Player API must return only information currently allowed for that player and playthrough.\r\n Do not return future chapter content, unrevealed chapter names, hidden location names, future artifact records, solution \r\ntext, Captain notes, internal tags, branching conditions, unpublished media, or authoring metadata.\r\n Do not include hidden asset URLs in HTML, preload manifests, source maps, CSS variables, serialized page state, or network \r\nresponses.\r\n Authorize every media request. A guessed image URL must not bypass reveal state.\r\n Use public-safe slugs or opaque IDs for Player routes. Never rely on sequential IDs as protection.\r\n For unauthorized protected resources, prefer a safe 404 or generic access-denied response that does not confirm the \r\nexistence of hidden Tales or assets.\r\nRequired middleware/policy checks\r\nauthorize(principal, action, resource) must verify:\r\n1. principal is authenticated for the requested workspace\r\n2. principal has the required global or Tale-scoped role\r\n3. resource belongs to an authorized Tall Tale or playthrough\r\n4. requested action is valid for the current resource state\r\n5. field-level reveal rules permit returned data\r\n6. asset/realtime/export scope matches the same authorization decision\r\n7. the decision is logged when it changes story state or access\r\nSecurity controls\r\n Use secure, HttpOnly, SameSite cookies when compatible with the deployment topology.\r\n Use CSRF protection for cookie-authenticated mutations.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 8 =====\r\nTALL TALE PLATFORM\r\nCODEX IMPLEMENTATION SPECIFICATION  |  JULY 17, 2026 PAGE 8\r\n Hash passwords with Argon2id or the strongest supported modern algorithm in the existing stack.\r\n Hash invitation tokens at rest; store only a prefix or identifier for support display.\r\n Rate-limit sign-in, invitation redemption, code lookup, password reset, and token validation.\r\n Rotate sessions after authentication and privilege changes.\r\n Revoke active sessions when a Player is removed from a playthrough or a Captain assignment is withdrawn, according to \r\npolicy.\r\n Do not place secrets or authorization claims in analytics, logs, query strings, or client error reports.\r\nThreat scenarios Codex must explicitly test\r\n A signed-in Player manually enters Captain and Studio URLs.\r\n A Player changes playthrough, Tale, chapter, player, invitation, or asset IDs in requests.\r\n A Player inspects page source, hydration state, network responses, prefetches, and browser caches for spoilers.\r\n A revoked invitation is redeemed from a previously opened tab.\r\n A removed Player continues using an old session or realtime connection.\r\n A Captain without Creator permission calls Studio APIs directly.\r\n A Creator without Captain assignment attempts to operate a live voyage.\r\n A user replays a captured mutation request after the resource state has changed.\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

[{"text": "Script completed\nWall time 0.4 seconds\nOutput:\n", "type": "input_text"}, {"text": "Exit code: 0\nWall time: 0.4 seconds\nOutput:\n\r\n===== PAGE 9 =====\r\nTALL TALE PLATFORM\r\nCODEX IMPLEMENTATION SPECIFICATION  |  JULY 17, 2026 PAGE 9\r\n07  PLAYER TALL TALE LIBRARY\r\nThe Player Library is a first-class destination and lasting personal archive, not a thin list of launch links.\r\nLibrary content model\r\nEvery Player must be able to see all Tall Tales to which they have been invited, added, or granted access. The library must group \r\nitems by playthrough state while still allowing multiple invitations or playthroughs for the same Tall Tale. A Tall Tale is the \r\nauthored work; a playthrough is one historical voyage through a specific version.\r\nLibrary group Included records Primary action\r\nInvitations Pending invitations not yet accepted or declined View Invitation / Accept / Decline\r\nAwaiting Captain Accepted or assigned playthroughs not yet launched Open Waiting Room\r\nIn Progress Launched, paused, or actively playable voyages Continue the Tale\r\nCompleted Completed playthroughs grouped by Tall Tale and run Open Voyage Archive\r\nReplay / New Edition Only replay or newer-version opportunities explicitly offered to this \r\nPlayer Accept Replay / Begin New Voyage\r\nExpired or Revoked Optional history view when useful and safe View status; no access to protected \r\nTale content\r\nTall Tale card requirements\r\n Artwork: use the approved Player-safe cover or invitation artwork. Never use unrevealed chapter art as a card background.\r\n Identity: Tall Tale title, spoiler-safe subtitle, Captain display name, and voyage/playthrough label.\r\n State: invited, awaiting Captain, in progress, paused, completed, expired, or revoked. Status must come from backend state.\r\n Progress: show only player-safe progress such as chapter count revealed, current revealed chapter, or percentage derived \r\nfrom allowed checkpoints. Never reveal future chapter names.\r\n History: for completed runs, show completion date, exact version label, ending/route only when revealed, and number of \r\nmemories collected.\r\n Action: one clear primary action plus a restrained overflow menu for safe secondary actions.\r\nCompleted Tall Tales and historical versions\r\nCompleted Tall Tales must remain in the Player Library permanently unless the Player chooses to hide them from their default \r\nshelf. Do not overwrite the completed record when the Creator publishes a later version. Each completed playthrough must \r\nopen a Voyage Archive bound to the exact version snapshot that was played.\r\n Group multiple completed playthroughs under the same Tall Tale while preserving each run separately.\r\n Display the published version label and publication date used for that run.\r\n Allow the Player to review only the historical content actually revealed during that playthrough, plus post-completion \r\ncontent intentionally released by the Captain or Creator.\r\n Preserve collected photos, artifacts, journal pages, clues, decisions, side quests, hint history when player-visible, ending, \r\ncompletion date, and optional Captain epilogue.\r\n If a later edition exists, do not silently replace the archive. Show a New Edition Available state only when the Player has been \r\ninvited, assigned, or explicitly granted replay access.\r\n If the same Tall Tale is replayed, create another playthrough record rather than resetting or mutating the completed one.\r\nHISTORICAL TRUTH\r\nA Player's completed archive is evidence of what they actually experienced. Treat it as an immutable historical record, \r\nnot a dynamic view of whatever the Creator happens to be editing today.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 10 =====\r\nTALL TALE PLATFORM\r\nCODEX IMPLEMENTATION SPECIFICATION  |  JULY 17, 2026 PAGE 10\r\n08  PLAYER LIBRARY EXPERIENCE DETAILS\r\nThe Player Library must remain beautiful, clear, spoiler-safe, and practical on desktop and phone.\r\nDefault presentation\r\nUse a rich gallery view by default, with an optional compact list view when the library grows. Cards should feel like journals, \r\nsealed letters, map cases, or archive volumes, but the visual metaphor must never reduce readability or make status difficult to \r\nidentify.\r\nVisual states\r\nState Visual treatment Required truth signal\r\nInvited Sealed letter or closed journal with sender seal Invitation expiration and acceptance state\r\nAwaiting Captain Closed journal with subtle living ambience Accepted and ready; Captain has not \r\nlaunched\r\nIn Progress Open journal, bookmark, active compass, subtle reflected light Current player-safe checkpoint and last \r\nsynchronized time\r\nPaused Bookmark or tied ribbon, reduced motion Who paused it and whether the Player may \r\ncontinue\r\nCompleted Bound archive volume or keepsake box Completion date and exact Tale version\r\nRevoked/Expired Dimmed but legible, no false danger styling Access is no longer valid and why, when \r\nsafe to disclose\r\nPlayer filters and search\r\n Filter by Invited, Awaiting Captain, In Progress, Completed, and Hidden/Archived.\r\n Search by Tall Tale title, Captain display name, voyage label, completion year, or player-safe tag.\r\n Sort by recently active, recently invited, completion date, title, or manually pinned favorites.\r\n Permit hiding a completed Tale from the main shelf without deleting its historical record.\r\n Do not show Creator-only tags, internal status names, draft identifiers, or unrevealed location metadata in search indexes.\r\nEmpty states\r\n No Tall Tales yet: explain how invitation links and codes work, with a single Enter Invitation Code action.\r\n No active voyages: show completed memories or pending invitations before suggesting that there is nothing to do.\r\n No completed voyages: use encouraging world-consistent copy without inventing progress.\r\n Offline or stale data: show last successful synchronization time and make it explicit that status may be outdated.\r\nPlayer privacy\r\nA Player should see their own Tall Tales and crews according to membership. Do not expose other players' private profiles, email \r\naddresses, invitation tokens, device information, or Captain operational notes. For crew Tales, show only the crew identity fields \r\nexplicitly approved for Player display.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 11 =====\r\nTALL TALE PLATFORM\r\nCODEX IMPLEMENTATION SPECIFICATION  |  JULY 17, 2026 PAGE 11\r\n09  INVITATION ACCEPTANCE AND PLAYER WAITING \r\nROOM\r\nThe invitation journey should feel ceremonial while remaining fast, secure, and recoverable.\r\nInvitation landing page\r\nOpening a valid invitation link should focus the gateway on the Player path. Reveal a themed invitation page with Tall Tale \r\nartwork, spoiler-safe description, Captain identity, scheduled start information, and a clear acceptance action. Do not expose the \r\nraw token in rendered copy, analytics, or support screenshots.\r\n1. Validate the invitation token server-side before returning Tale metadata.\r\n2. Display the invitation recipient name when known, with a safe correction path if the Captain misspelled it.\r\n3. Request the configured PIN/password only after the invitation identity is established.\r\n4. Allow the Player to sign in to an existing account or continue as a guest profile if the invitation permits it.\r\n5. Record acceptance atomically and prevent duplicate membership creation on refresh or double-click.\r\n6. Add the playthrough to the Player Library immediately.\r\n7. Enter the waiting room when the voyage is not yet launched.\r\nWaiting room\r\n Show a closed journal or sealed voyage object with ambient motion.\r\n Display truthful readiness state: accepted, awaiting other players, awaiting Captain, scheduled for a specific time, or paused \r\nbefore launch.\r\n Offer audio test, accessibility preferences, device readiness, and a reconnect action.\r\n Do not expose chapter count, future locations, internal readiness issues, or Captain controls.\r\n When the Captain launches the voyage, transition into the journal opening sequence through realtime update or a short \r\npolling fallback.\r\n If realtime connection fails, show a visible reconnect state and allow manual refresh without losing acceptance.\r\nDecline, expiration, and revocation\r\nPlayers must be able to decline an invitation without deleting their account. Expired or revoked invitations must never be \r\nredeemable, but the Player may see a safe explanatory state and request a replacement through an approved channel. The \r\nsystem must distinguish expired, revoked, already used, invalid, and unauthorized without leaking sensitive Tale existence to \r\narbitrary callers.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 12 =====\r\nTALL TALE PLATFORM\r\nCODEX IMPLEMENTATION SPECIFICATION  |  JULY 17, 2026 PAGE 12\r\n10  CAPTAIN LIBRARY AND COMMAND WORKSPACE\r\nThe Captain Library is the operational center for running voyages, not the identity.\r\n Exact published version.\r\n Current player-safe checkpoint and current Captain-facing operational checkpoint.\r\n Last activity and synchronization health.\r\n Pending Player actions only when valid for the current state.\r\nCaptain details page\r\nThe Captain-facing Tall Tale details page should summarize published versions, active voyages, invited players, completed It must not mix draft editing controls directly into the live Captain console.\r\nSEPARATION OF CONCERNS\r\nCaptain runs the performance. Creator builds the stage. The same human may hold both roles, but the interfaces must \r\nremain distinct so live operation is not surrounded by dangerous authoring controls.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 13 =====\r\nTALL TALE PLATFORM\r\nCODEX IMPLEMENTATION SPECIFICATION  |  JULY 17, 2026 PAGE 13\r\n11  CLEAN CAPTAIN INVITATION WORKFLOW\r\nInviting a Player must be one of the most polished flows in the platform.\r\nEntry points\r\n Invite Players from a published Tall Tale card.\r\n Start New Voyage from a Tall Tale details page.\r\n Invite from the Captain Library global action.\r\n Duplicate a completed playthrough configuration into a new invitation without copying historical progress.\r\nInvitation wizard\r\nStep Required fields and decisions\r\n1. Select Tale Tall Tale, published version, starting point, solo/crew, normal/test voyage.\r\n2. Configure Voyage Voyage name, Captain, progression mode, hints, side quests, schedule, accessibility defaults.\r\n3. Add Players Existing Player, new guest profile, email when supported, display name, crew role, optional per-player PIN.\r\n4. Security Expiration, redemption limit, account requirement, password/PIN, device/session policy.\r\n5. Delivery Secure link, QR code, short code, copyable message, email when configured.\r\n6. Review Player-safe preview, version lock, schedule, invitation summary, validation warnings.\r\n7. Create Atomic playthrough and invitation creation; then show management screen.\r\nVoyage configuration\r\n Voyage name: human-readable and unique enough for Captain and Player libraries. Default from Player name plus Tale title \r\nbut allow editing.\r\n Published version: required for real voyages. Once launched, the playthrough remains bound to that version.\r\n Starting point: normally the beginning; permit later chapter starts only when the published Tale and Captain policy allow it.\r\n Progression mode: Captain-controlled, rule-controlled, or hybrid. Existing manual behavior must remain supported.\r\n Hints: disabled, available on request, Captain-pushed, timed, or Tale-defined.\r\n Side quests: enabled according to published version and voyage configuration.\r\n Schedule: optional planned start time with explicit timezone and no false guarantee of Captain presence.\r\n Replay mode: creates a new playthrough while optionally carrying Player-safe preferences, never prior progress.\r\nReview screen\r\nBefore creation, show exactly what the Player will see and flag any invalid or dangerous configuration. Prevent launch when the \r\nselected version is unpublished, missing required Player content, references inaccessible media, or contains unresolved story-\r\ngraph errors.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 14 =====\r\nTALL TALE PLATFORM\r\nCODEX IMPLEMENTATION SPECIFICATION  |  JULY 17, 2026 PAGE 14\r\n12  INVITATION DELIVERY, LIFECYCLE, AND \r\nMANAGEMENT\r\nEvery invitation is a tracked security object, not merely a URL copied from the browser.\r\nDelivery methods\r\nMethod Phase-one requirement Notes\r\nSecure link Required Opaque token; copy button; themed invitation landing page.\r\nQR code Required Encodes the secure link; themed printable/display card; fallback short code.\r\nShort code Required Human-friendly code entered from Player gateway; rate-limited lookup.\r\nCopyable message Required Captain can copy a polished invitation message containing the link and safe \r\ndetails.\r\nEmail Optional initially Add when outbound email infrastructure exists; do not block core invitation \r\nflow.\r\nInvitation states\r\nDRAFT\r\n  -> CREATED\r\n  -> SENT or COPIED\r\n  -> VIEWED\r\n  -> ACCEPTED\r\n  -> JOINED / READY\r\n  -> CONSUMED (when single-use policy applies)\r\nAny non-final valid state may become:\r\n  -> EXPIRED\r\n  -> REVOKED\r\n  -> REPLACED\r\nDECLINED is a player decision and must remain distinct from REVOKED.\r\nCaptain invitation dashboard\r\n Show recipient, Tale, voyage, published version, status, expiration, delivery methods, viewed time, accepted time, and \r\nreplacement lineage.\r\n Provide Copy Link, Show QR, Copy Message, Extend Expiration, Revoke, Replace, and Open Player actions as permitted.\r\n Do not display raw tokens after creation. Regenerate/replace instead of revealing stored secrets.\r\n A replacement invitation must invalidate the original according to policy and preserve the audit relationship between them.\r\n Bulk invitations for crews must produce individual invitation identities unless the Captain deliberately chooses a shared crew \r\nlink with clearly weaker accountability.\r\nToken requirements\r\n Generate cryptographically secure random tokens with enough entropy for public links.\r\n Store only a one-way hash of the token, plus a non-secret lookup prefix if needed.\r\n Support expiration, revocation time, accepted time, redemption count, maximum redemptions, and last validation time.\r\n Bind the invitation to Tall Tale, published version, playthrough, intended player/crew, and allowed scope.\r\n Do not allow changing the bound version after acceptance without creating a new invitation or explicit safe migration flow.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 15 =====\r\nTALL TALE PLATFORM\r\nCODEX IMPLEMENTATION SPECIFICATION  |  JULY 17, 2026 PAGE 15\r\n13  CREATOR WORKSPACE AND STUDIO INTEGRATION\r\nCreator is a distinct workspace even when the same account is also the Captain.\r\nCreator entry\r\nSelecting Creator on the gateway must authenticate the account and open the Studio Library when the account has Creator \r\npermission. A Captain/Creator account may switch between Captain's Command and Tall Tale Studio through an authenticated \r\nworkspace switcher. The Player interface must not expose that switcher.\r\nStudio Library\r\nGroup Contents Primary action\r\nDrafts Unpublished working copies and incomplete Tales Continue Editing\r\nReady to Publish Validated drafts meeting publication rules Review and Publish\r\nPublished Tall Tales with one or more immutable versions Open Details / Create New \r\nDraft\r\nNeeds Attention Broken references, missing media, unsafe Player exposure, migration \r\nconflicts Resolve Issues\r\nArchived Retired Tales retained for history Restore or View History\r\nPublishing rules\r\n Publishing creates an immutable Tall Tale version snapshot.\r\n Editing a published Tale creates or updates a working draft; it never mutates the published snapshot.\r\n A draft may be based on a prior version and must record that ancestry.\r\n Publication validation must check story graph integrity, required Player content, asset availability, access scopes, Captain \r\ncontrols, recovery paths, and Player-safe projections.\r\n Publishing must produce release notes or a structured diff summary.\r\n Active playthroughs remain on their bound version unless an explicit, audited migration is supported and safe.\r\nPreview modes\r\n Player preview: uses a temporary preview principal and synthetic reveal state. It must be visually marked and must not \r\nmutate live progress.\r\n Captain preview: shows operational controls for test data only or a non-mutating simulation.\r\n Version preview: renders the exact immutable snapshot, not the current draft.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 16 =====\r\nTALL TALE PLATFORM\r\nCODEX IMPLEMENTATION SPECIFICATION  |  JULY 17, 2026 PAGE 16\r\n14  TALL TALE AND PLAYTHROUGH VERSIONING\r\nAuthored history and played history are separate and both must be preserved.\r\nCore definitions\r\nEntity Meaning Mutability\r\nTallTale Long-lived identity, ownership, public metadata, lifecycle Mutable metadata under \r\npolicy\r\nTallTaleDraft Current editable working copy Mutable with revision \r\ntracking\r\nTallTaleVersion Published complete snapshot Immutable\r\nPlaythrough One voyage by one Player or crew State progresses; identity \r\npersists\r\nPlaythroughMembership Player/Captain relationship to a playthrough Mutable under authorization \r\nwith history\r\nPlaythroughEvent Append-only state/history event Immutable or correction-by-\r\ncompensating-event\r\nMemoryArchive Player-facing completed historical projection Immutable source data; \r\npresentation may evolve\r\nVersion lock behavior\r\n1. A Captain selects an immutable TallTaleVersion while configuring the voyage.\r\n2. The Playthrough stores that version ID before invitations are issued.\r\n3. Invitation metadata and Player-safe previews are generated from that version.\r\n4. Launching the voyage permanently locks the binding for normal operation.\r\n5. Publishing a later version does not affect the active or completed playthrough.\r\n6. A replay creates a new Playthrough bound to the selected version.\r\n7. An exceptional migration must be explicit, audited, compatibility-checked, reversible where practical, and never silently \r\nrewrite completed history.\r\nVersion comparison\r\n Compare metadata, chapters, story blocks, branching conditions, media, artifacts, Captain instructions, Player content, \r\nrecovery logic, and configuration defaults.\r\n Classify changes as added, removed, modified, moved, renamed, or access-scope changed.\r\n Flag changes that would break active playthrough compatibility.\r\n Allow restore by creating a new draft based on the selected historical version. Never reopen the immutable version for \r\nediting.\r\n Allow fork into a new Tall Tale identity while preserving provenance.\r\nPlayer-visible historical version\r\nThe Player may see the version label and a completed archive reconstructed from the exact snapshot and reveal history. The \r\nPlayer does not automatically receive the Creator's full authoring diff, hidden content, internal version notes, or versions they \r\nnever played or were never offered.\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

Encode legal transitions and enforce them in domain services and persistence constraints \r\nwhere practical.\r\nPlaythrough states\r\nDRAFT_SETUP\r\n  -> INVITING\r\n  -> READY\r\n  -> SCHEDULED (optional)\r\n  -> ACTIVE\r\n  -> PAUSED\r\n  -> ACTIVE\r\n  -> COMPLETED\r\nDRAFT_SETUP / INVITING / READY / SCHEDULED may become CANCELLED.\r\nACTIVE / PAUSED may become ABANDONED only through an ACCEPTED / READY -> REMOVED\r\nAny non-terminal membership may become SUSPENDED under policy.\r\nTall Tale lifecycle\r\nDRAFT -> VALIDATING -> READY_TO_PUBLISH -> PUBLISHED\r\nPUBLISHED -> NEW_DRAFT (based on published version)\r\nPUBLISHED -> ARCHIVED\r\nARCHIVED -> RESTORED (creates active metadata state; versions remain immutable)\r\nTransition enforcement\r\n Validate actor permission and current state in one transaction.\r\n Use optimistic concurrency or row/version locking to prevent double launch, double acceptance, and conflicting Captain \r\nactions.\r\n Record a playthrough or audit event for meaningful transitions.\r\n Return structured conflict responses when state changed since the client loaded.\r\n Make retry-safe mutations idempotent where network retries are expected.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 19 =====\r\nTALL TALE PLATFORM\r\nCODEX IMPLEMENTATION SPECIFICATION  |  JULY 17, 2026 PAGE 19\r\n17  BACKEND SERVICES AND API CONTRACTS\r\nUse domain-focused services rather than scattering permission and state-transition logic through route handlers.\r\nRecommended service boundaries\r\nService Responsibilities\r\nAuthenticationService Sign-in, session rotation, projections, lifecycle, archive/restore.\r\nDraftService Editable draft graph, autosave, validation, revision conflicts.\r\nPublishingService Validation, immutable snapshot, checksum, release notes, replace, expire.\r\nPlayerLibraryService Authorized grouped library projection and search.\r\nCaptainLibraryService Needs-attention and operational projections.\r\nAssetService Upload metadata, scoped access, signed delivery, historical version binding.\r\nRealtimeService Authorized playthrough channels, reconnect, event sequence recovery.\r\nAuditService Security and state-change event capture.\r\nRepresentative API capabilities\r\nGET    /api/player/library\r\nGET    /api/player/tales/:taleId\r\nGET    /api/player/playthroughs/:playthroughId\r\nGET    /api/player/playthroughs/:playthroughId/archive\r\nPOST   /api/invitations/resolve\r\nPOST   /api/invitations/:token/accept\r\nPOST   /api/invitations/:token/decline\r\nGET    /api/captain/library\r\nPOST   /api/captain/playthroughs\r\nPOST   /api/captain/playthroughs/:id/invitations\r\nPOST   /api/captain/playthroughs/:id/launch\r\nPOST   /api/captain/playthroughs/:id/pause\r\nPOST   /api/captain/playthroughs/:id/resume\r\nPOST   /api/captain/playthroughs/:id/actions/:action\r\nGET    /api/studio/library\r\nPOST   /api/studio/tales/:id/drafts\r\nPOST   /api/studio/tales/:id/validate\r\nPOST   /api/studio/tales/:id/publish\r\nGET    /api/studio/tales/:id/versions\r\nGET    /api/studio/tales/:id/versions/compare\r\nResponse design\r\n Return explicit view models per workspace instead of raw ORM entities.\r\n Include stable status codes and machine-readable state, plus user-facing copy keys or safe messages.\r\n Include server timestamps and event sequence/version numbers for synchronization truth.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 20 =====\r\nTALL TALE PLATFORM\r\nCODEX IMPLEMENTATION SPECIFICATION  |  JULY 17, 2026 PAGE 20\r\n Do not return data merely because the frontend currently ignores it.\r\n Use pagination for large libraries, invitation history, audit history, and completed playthroughs.\r\n18  REALTIME SYNCHRONIZATION AND INTERFACE \r\nTRUTH\r\nCaptain and Player screens must agree about what has actually happened.\r\nRealtime events\r\n Invitation viewed, accepted, declined, expired, revoked, and replaced.\r\n Player joined waiting room, disconnected, reconnected, or became ready.\r\n Captain launched, paused, resumed, advanced, revealed, sent hint, or completed the voyage.\r\n Player reached a checkpoint, requested a hint, acknowledged a reveal, or completed a required action.\r\n Version or configuration conflict requiring Captain attention.\r\nTransport behavior\r\nUse the existing realtime technology when present. Otherwise use WebSocket or server-sent events with authenticated channel \r\nsubscription, plus a conservative polling fallback. Realtime connection membership must be authorized against the playthrough \r\non every connection and reauthorization boundary.\r\nTruthful status requirements\r\n Never display Updated just now continuously unless a real update occurred.\r\n Show last server-confirmed synchronization time, connection state, and pending local action when relevant.\r\n Use event sequence numbers or version tokens to detect missed updates and refetch authoritative state.\r\n Do not optimistically claim a Captain reveal or Player acceptance succeeded until the server confirms it.\r\n If a mutation is pending, say so explicitly. If it failed, restore the prior state and explain the failure.\r\nINTERFACE TRUTH RULE\r\nAnimations may anticipate a confirmed state only after the backend commits it. The platform must never use visual \r\nconfidence to disguise uncertain or stale data.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 21 =====\r\nTALL TALE PLATFORM\r\nCODEX IMPLEMENTATION SPECIFICATION  |  JULY 17, 2026 PAGE 21\r\n19  MOTION, SOUND, AND CINEMATIC TRANSITIONS\r\nAnimations are part of the product, but they must be state-driven, interruptible, and accessible.\r\nMotion architecture\r\n Create named transition sequences for gateway arrival, role selection, sign-in transition, library arrival, invitation opening, \r\nwaiting-room launch, journal opening, workspace switching, and voyage completion.\r\n Drive sequences from application state rather than arbitrary timeouts.\r\n Use a transition controller capable of cancellation, skip, reverse/return behavior, reduced-motion substitution, and cleanup \r\non unmount/navigation.\r\n Preload only assets safe for the current user and current reveal state.\r\n Avoid long empty waits. Longer sequences must contain meaningful staged motion and allow a skip or fast-return path.\r\nAudio behavior\r\n Respect browser autoplay policies and existing user audio preference.\r\n Provide visible mute/volume controls and remember preference appropriately.\r\n Use ambience and object sounds sparingly; do not make every hover clang like a hardware store collapsing.\r\n Stop or reduce ambience when the page is hidden or the user enters a content-heavy accessibility mode.\r\nReduced-motion equivalents\r\nWhen reduced motion is enabled, replace large camera travel, parallax, page flips, and continuous drift with short crossfades, \r\ndirect state changes, and restrained emphasis. Preserve hierarchy, confirmation, and story meaning without forcing motion.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 22 =====\r\nTALL TALE PLATFORM\r\nCODEX IMPLEMENTATION SPECIFICATION  |  JULY 17, 2026 PAGE 22\r\n20  RESPONSIVE DESIGN AND DEVICE BEHAVIOR\r\nThe platform must support desktop, laptop, tablet, and phone without treating mobile as a shrunken desktop.\r\nGateway responsiveness\r\n Desktop: role objects may share one cinematic scene and respond to pointer movement.\r\n Tablet: retain depth but reduce simultaneous effects and ensure touch targets are generous.\r\n Phone portrait: present role objects as a vertical cinematic sequence or carousel with clear labels; do not cram three tiny \r\nobjects across the screen.\r\n Landscape phone: preserve safe-area insets and avoid role controls behind browser UI.\r\nLibrary responsiveness\r\n Desktop: gallery plus optional list view, persistent filters where space allows.\r\n Phone: single-column cards, sticky state-aware primary action, bottom-sheet filters, and concise metadata.\r\n Keep completed historical archives readable on phone, including photos, journal pages, and timeline events.\r\n Do not hide critical state or authorization failures inside hover-only interactions.\r\nCaptain operating mode\r\nCaptain controls must be usable from a second monitor, laptop, or phone during gameplay. Prioritize clear current state, large \r\nsafe action targets, confirmation for irreversible actions, and minimal navigation depth. Do not require the Captain to enter \r\nStudio to operate a live voyage.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 23 =====\r\nTALL TALE PLATFORM\r\nCODEX IMPLEMENTATION SPECIFICATION  |  JULY 17, 2026 PAGE 23\r\n21  ACCESSIBILITY\r\nCinematic presentation must not exclude keyboard, screen-reader, low-vision, motion-sensitive, or touch-only users.\r\n[ ] Every role choice is a semantic button or link with an accessible name and description.\r\n[ ] Keyboard focus order follows the visual order and remains visible against animated backgrounds.\r\n[ ] All dialogs trap focus correctly and return focus to the triggering control.\r\n[ ] Status is not communicated by color or animation alone.\r\n[ ] Invitation QR codes always have a human-readable code and link alternative.\r\n[ ] Forms have persistent labels, helpful errors, and no placeholder-only identification.\r\n[ ] Screen readers receive meaningful live-region updates for invitation acceptance, Captain launch, and synchronization state \r\nwithout excessive announcements.\r\n[ ] Reduced motion is honored automatically and can also be selected in application preferences.\r\n[ ] Text remains readable at 200% zoom and with browser font scaling.\r\n[ ] Color contrast meets WCAG AA for essential text and controls.\r\n[ ] Touch targets are at least the platform-appropriate accessible minimum.\r\n[ ] Historical photos and story images support authored alt text and decorative classification.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 24 =====\r\nTALL TALE PLATFORM\r\nCODEX IMPLEMENTATION SPECIFICATION  |  JULY 17, 2026 PAGE 24\r\n22  LOADING, ERROR, CONFLICT, AND RECOVERY \r\nSTATES\r\nThe platform must behave gracefully when networks, sessions, humans, and browsers perform their customary acts of sabotage.\r\nLoading\r\n Use scene-consistent skeletons or staged reveals, but do not show fake progress values.\r\n Load Player-safe library metadata before optional decorative assets.\r\n Keep sign-in and invitation redemption controls responsive while nonessential ambience loads.\r\nErrors\r\nCondition Required behavior\r\nInvalid/expired invitation Safe explanation, no Tale leak, return to Player gateway, optional request-replacement guidance.\r\nUnauthorized route Generic access-denied or safe 404; no protected data in page source.\r\nSession expired Preserve safe intended destination and return to matching sign-in.\r\nVersion conflict Explain that state changed; refetch; do not overwrite newer state.\r\nRealtime disconnected Show disconnected/stale indicator, retry, and authoritative refresh.\r\nAsset unavailable Use a designed fallback and log actionable diagnostics; do not expose storage internals.\r\nCaptain action failed Do not animate success; restore controls; show retryable error with correlation ID when \r\nappropriate.\r\nRecovery\r\n Invitation acceptance must be idempotent across refresh and repeated submission.\r\n Incomplete voyage setup must save as a resumable draft rather than creating orphaned records.\r\n If QR generation fails, the secure link and short code remain available.\r\n If Player launch synchronization is missed, the Player can refresh and enter the correct active state.\r\n If a Captain accidentally navigates away, the live voyage remains authoritative on the server and reopens to the current state.\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

IMPLEMENTATION SPECIFICATION  |  JULY 17, 2026 PAGE 25\r\n23  AUDIT HISTORY AND OPERATIONAL DIAGNOSTICS\r\nMeaningful access and story-control actions must be traceable without storing secrets or spoiling the Player experience.\r\nAudit events\r\n Authentication success/failure, session creation, logout, role denial, and privilege change.\r\n Invitation created, copied/sent, viewed, accepted, declined, expired, revoked, replaced, and redeemed.\r\n Player added, removed, suspended, restored, or account-claimed.\r\n Playthrough created, configured, launched, paused, resumed, archived, or unarchived.\r\n Protected asset access denial and suspicious code/token attempts when useful for security monitoring.\r\nAudit design\r\n Record actor, action, resource, outcome, timestamp, correlation ID, and safe metadata.\r\n Never record raw passwords, PINs, invitation tokens, session cookies, or complete private story payloads.\r\n Provide Captain-visible operational history only for authorized Tales and voyages.\r\n Keep security diagnostics separate from Player-facing story history.\r\n Use retention appropriate to the project and document it.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 26 =====\r\nTALL TALE PLATFORM\r\nCODEX IMPLEMENTATION SPECIFICATION  |  JULY 17, 2026 PAGE 26\r\n24  MIGRATION FROM THE CURRENT APPLICATION\r\nPreserve current Player, Captain, Studio, Tall Tale, and password behavior while moving to the unified model.\r\nRepository discovery before migration\r\n[ ] Identify existing Player login/password records and how a Player is currently associated with a Tall Tale.\r\n[ ] Identify Captain authentication and whether Captain and Studio already share credentials.\r\n[ ] Map every current Tall Tale record, chapter, clue, media asset, progress field, and Captain control to the new domain \r\nmodel.\r\n[ ] Identify active sessions and current URLs that must redirect safely.\r\n[ ] Inventory existing animations and reusable visual assets.\r\n[ ] Identify whether current progress is mutable content state, event history, or both.\r\nBackfill strategy\r\n1. Create TallTale identities for all existing Tales.\r\n2. Create an initial immutable published version from the current playable content of each Tale.\r\n3. Create a working draft based on that version only when current unpublished edits exist.\r\n4. Create PlayerProfile records for existing Player identities or password-bound Player entries.\r\n5. Create Playthrough records for current active and completed Tall Tale progress.\r\n6. Bind each migrated playthrough to the generated initial published version.\r\n7. Create historical PlaythroughEvents or a clearly marked migration snapshot event sufficient to preserve current state.\r\n8. Create RoleAssignments for existing Captain and Studio accounts.\r\n9. Redirect old Player/Captain/Studio routes to the new route families while preserving intended destination.\r\nCompatibility\r\n Existing Player passwords must continue to work during the transition unless intentionally reset through a visible migration \r\nflow.\r\n Existing active Tall Tales must resume at the same progress point.\r\n Existing Captain controls must not disappear before their replacements are functional.\r\n Do not expose migrated drafts or Captain notes to Players due to a broad serialization shortcut.\r\n Provide a rollback plan for database migration and a dry-run validation report.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 27 =====\r\nTALL TALE PLATFORM\r\nCODEX IMPLEMENTATION SPECIFICATION  |  JULY 17, 2026 PAGE 27\r\n25  IMPLEMENTATION PHASES\r\nBuild in coherent vertical slices so each phase produces a usable, testable platform increment.\r\nPhase 1 - Domain foundation and access control\r\n[ ] Repository inventory and architecture decision record.\r\n[ ] Account, role assignment, Player profile, Tale, version, playthrough, membership, invitation, event, and asset-scope model \r\nchanges.\r\n[ ] Server-side authorization service and protected route middleware.\r\n[ ] Initial Tall Tale version snapshot and migration/backfill tooling.\r\n[ ] Compatibility tests for current routes and passwords.\r\nPhase 2 - Gateway and authentication\r\n[ ] Cinematic role gateway with Player, Captain, and Creator options.\r\n[ ] Player invitation/direct sign-in flow.\r\n[ ] Captain/Creator sign-in and authorized workspace selection.\r\n[ ] Session restore, logout, expiry, and safe redirects.\r\n[ ] Reduced-motion and responsive variants.\r\nPhase 3 - Player Tall Tale Library\r\n[ ] Invited, awaiting Captain, active, paused, completed, and replay/new-edition groups.\r\n[ ] Card states, search, filters, list/gallery modes, empty states.\r\n[ ] Completed Voyage Archive bound to exact version.\r\n[ ] Multiple playthrough grouping and permanent historical access.\r\n[ ] Player-safe data and asset projections.\r\nPhase 4 - Captain Library and invitations\r\n[ ] Captain Library groups and needs-attention projection.\r\n[ ] Playthrough setup and invitation wizard.\r\n[ ] Secure link, QR, short code, copyable message.\r\n[ ] Invitation dashboard, lifecycle actions, and audit history.\r\n[ ] Waiting room and Captain launch synchronization.\r\nPhase 5 - Studio integration and publishing\r\n[ ] Creator gateway and Studio Library.\r\n[ ] Immutable publishing service, validation, release notes, version comparison.\r\n[ ] Preview modes and workspace switching.\r\n[ ] Restore-to-draft and fork operations.\r\nPhase 6 - polish, diagnostics, and hardening\r\n[ ] Full cinematic transitions and sound behavior.\r\n[ ] Authorization penetration tests and asset-leak tests.\r\n[ ] Performance, offline/stale-state, reconnect, and concurrency testing.\r\n[ ] Accessibility audit and reduced-motion validation.\r\n[ ] Documentation, migration report, and final acceptance walkthrough.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 28 =====\r\nTALL TALE PLATFORM\r\nCODEX IMPLEMENTATION SPECIFICATION  |  JULY 17, 2026 PAGE 28\r\n26  TESTING STRATEGY\r\nTests must prove both beautiful journeys and ugly edge cases, because software mainly encounters the latter.\r\nUnit tests\r\n Role and scoped authorization policies.\r\n Player-safe projection and field redaction.\r\n Legal/illegal state transitions.\r\n Invitation token hashing, expiration, revocation, replacement, and redemption limits.\r\n Version publication immutability and checksum validation.\r\n Library grouping and sorting.\r\n Replay creation without prior progress mutation.\r\nIntegration tests\r\n Database migrations and backfill against representative current data.\r\n Authentication plus route middleware.\r\n Invitation create-resolve-accept flow with retry/idempotency.\r\n Captain launch and Player realtime transition.\r\n Completed playthrough archive from immutable version and reveal history.\r\n Protected asset authorization with guessed IDs/URLs.\r\n Publishing while active playthroughs remain on prior version.\r\nEnd-to-end journeys\r\n1. New visitor selects Player, enters short code, accepts invitation, sees it in their library, waits, and enters when Captain \r\nlaunches.\r\n2. Returning Player signs in, sees invitations, active voyage, completed historical runs, and opens the exact version archive.\r\n3. Captain signs in, creates voyage, generates QR/link/code, sees invitation viewed and accepted, then launches.\r\n4. Creator signs in, edits draft, validates, publishes a new version, and confirms existing playthrough remains unchanged.\r\n5. Player manually enters a Captain URL and receives no protected data.\r\n6. Player guesses future asset and chapter IDs and receives no content.\r\n7. Two Captain devices attempt conflicting actions and receive correct concurrency handling.\r\n8. Invitation is revoked while open and cannot be accepted afterward.\r\nVisual and accessibility regression\r\n Desktop and phone screenshots for every major state.\r\n Keyboard-only gateway, login, library, invitation, and Captain flow.\r\n Screen-reader labels and live announcements.\r\n Reduced-motion snapshots and transition behavior.\r\n High zoom, long titles, missing art, many completed runs, and localization-length stress cases.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 29 =====\r\nTALL TALE PLATFORM\r\nCODEX IMPLEMENTATION SPECIFICATION  |  JULY 17, 2026 PAGE 29\r\n27  ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA\r\nThe work is complete only when the following observable outcomes are true.\r\nGateway\r\n[ ] The first page presents a polished game-like selection for Player, Captain, and Creator, or a documented two-role \r\npresentation with Creator accessible after Captain authentication.\r\n[ ] Selecting a role changes the sign-in path but never grants authorization.\r\n[ ] Returning sessions receive a truthful, fast continuation path.\r\n[ ] The gateway is responsive, keyboard accessible, and reduced-motion compatible.\r\nPlayer Library\r\n[ ] A Player can see every pending invitation and every Tall Tale to which they were added.\r\n[ ] A Player can distinguish awaiting-Captain, in-progress, paused, completed, expired, and revoked states.\r\n[ ] A Player can reopen completed Tall Tales and view each separate completed playthrough.\r\n[ ] Each completed playthrough displays and renders from the exact published Tall Tale version used for that run.\r\n[ ] Publishing a new version does not change any existing active or completed Player archive.\r\n[ ] The Player receives no Captain, Creator, future chapter, hidden asset, or unrevealed story data.\r\nCaptain invitations\r\n[ ] A Captain can create a playthrough from a published Tall Tale and select the version.\r\n[ ] A Captain can invite an existing or new Player using a secure link, QR code, and short code.\r\n[ ] Invitation status accurately progresses through viewed, accepted, ready, expired, revoked, or replaced.\r\n[ ] A Captain can revoke or replace an invitation without exposing stored tokens.\r\n[ ] A Player accepting an invitation is added to their library exactly once, even after refresh or retry.\r\n[ ] The Player waiting room transitions to the Tale only after an authoritative Captain launch.\r\nCreator and versions\r\n[ ] Creator access is separately authorized even when the account is also a Captain.\r\n[ ] Publishing creates an immutable version and does not mutate previous versions.\r\n[ ] Version comparison, restore-to-draft, and fork behavior preserve ancestry.\r\n[ ] Player preview and Captain preview cannot mutate live playthrough progress.\r\nSecurity and quality\r\n[ ] Protected routes, APIs, assets, exports, and realtime channels are server-authorized.\r\n[ ] No raw password, PIN, invitation token, or session secret is logged or stored improperly.\r\n[ ] Interface timestamps and synchronization states reflect real backend events.\r\n[ ] Automated tests cover authorization denial, token lifecycle, version lock, and historical archive behavior.\r\n[ ] No placeholder controls, dead navigation, fake statuses, or unfinished invitation actions remain.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 30 =====\r\nTALL TALE PLATFORM\r\nCODEX IMPLEMENTATION SPECIFICATION  |  JULY 17, 2026 PAGE 30\r\n28  CODEX EXECUTION models and asset delivery.\r\n[ ] Implement gateway, libraries, invitation flow, versioning, and realtime behavior.\r\n[ ] Preserve current working functionality through adapters and redirects.\r\n[ ] Add tests with each vertical slice rather than at the end.\r\n[ ] Keep design consistent with the existing Tall Tale visual language.\r\nBefore declaring completion\r\n[ ] Run formatters, linters, type checks, unit tests, integration tests, and end-to-end tests.\r\n[ ] Exercise all role journeys manually on desktop and phone-sized viewports.\r\n[ ] Verify Player cannot obtain Captain/Creator or spoiler data through URLs, APIs, assets, preload state, exports, or realtime \r\nchannels.\r\n[ ] Verify current Tall Tales, Player passwords, Captain access, and progress survived migration.\r\n[ ] Verify completed playthroughs remain bound to historical versions after publishing a new version.\r\n[ ] Update documentation and provide a concise implementation report including files changed, migrations, tests, remaining \r\nrisks, and screenshots where the project workflow supports them.\r\nCOMPLETION STANDARD\r\nDo not report the feature as complete because the pages exist. It is complete when the entire invitation-to-library-to-\r\nlaunch-to-completion-to-historical-archive journey works, permissions hold under hostile navigation, and the interface \r\nremains beautiful without lying about state.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 31 =====\r\nTALL TALE PLATFORM\r\nCODEX IMPLEMENTATION SPECIFICATION  |  JULY 17, 2026 PAGE 31\r\n29  REFERENCE USER JOURNEYS\r\nThese journeys define the intended product behavior in plain sequence form.\r\nJourney A - Invited Player\r\n1. The Captain creates Sera's Forever Treasure voyage from published version 1.0.\r\n2. The Captain generates a themed invitation link, QR card, and short code.\r\n3. The Player opens the link and sees the Player-focused invitation gateway.\r\n4. The Player confirms identity and enters the configured password or PIN.\r\n5. The invitation is accepted exactly once and appears in the Player Library.\r\n6. The Player enters an Awaiting Captain waiting room.\r\n7. The Captain sees the Player ready and launches the voyage.\r\n8. The Player receives the journal-opening transition and enters the active Tale.\r\nJourney B - Returning Player with history\r\n1. The Player signs in through the Player path.\r\n2. The Player Library shows one pending invitation, one active Tale, and two completed playthroughs.\r\n3. The Player continues the active Tale without seeing future chapter details.\r\n4. The Player opens a completed Tall Tale and selects the July 2026 playthrough.\r\n5. The Voyage Archive renders from version 1.0 and the exact reveal history of that run.\r\n6. A newer version 1.2 is not shown unless the Player has been offered or invited to it.\r\nJourney C - Captain creates and operates\r\n1. The Captain selects Captain at the gateway and signs in.\r\n2. Captain's Command shows a needs-attention card for a waiting Player.\r\n3. The Captain opens another published Tale and starts a new voyage.\r\n4. The invitation wizard validates version, Player, security, and delivery.\r\n5. The Captain copies the message and displays the QR code.\r\n6. Invitation status updates from viewed to accepted to ready.\r\n7. The Captain launches and later advances the Tale through authoritative controls.\r\nJourney D - Creator publishes without rewriting history\r\n1. The Creator selects Creator and signs in with the same account used for Captain access.\r\n2. The Studio opens the working draft based on version 1.0.\r\n3. The Creator adds a side quest, validates the story graph, and publishes version 1.1.\r\n4. Existing active and completed playthroughs remain on version 1.0.\r\n5. New voyages may select version 1.1.\r\n6. A Player sees a New Edition invitation only when the Captain creates one.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 32 =====\r\nTALL TALE PLATFORM\r\nCODEX IMPLEMENTATION SPECIFICATION  |  JULY 17, 2026 PAGE 32\r\n30  FINAL BUILD DIRECTIVE\r\nThis is the concise command that governs the entire implementation.\r\nBUILD DIRECTIVE\r\nImplement a unified Tall Tale platform in the existing repository with a cinematic Player/Captain/Creator entry \r\ngateway, secure role-aware authentication, a complete Player Tall Tale Library containing invitations, assigned Tales, \r\nactive voyages, completed voyages, and exact historical versions, a Captain Command library with polished invitation \r\ncreation and management, a separate Creator Studio workspace, immutable published versions, version-locked \r\nplaythroughs, realtime launch/progress synchronization, spoiler-safe data projections, protected assets, responsive \r\ncinematic design, accessibility, migrations, tests, and documentation. Preserve all current working behavior and data. \r\nDo not substitute hidden buttons for authorization, dynamic current drafts for historical truth, or decorative status text \r\nfor real backend state.\r\nDefinition of success\r\nA Player should feel as though they entered a living collection of adventures. A Captain should feel as though they entered a \r\nclear command center. A Creator should feel as though they entered a serious story-building studio. The database and backend \r\nshould quietly enforce identity, history, state, and secrecy underneath all three, because apparently even enchanted pirate \r\njournals require competent systems engineering.\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]
